title: The Chronicle of John Hardyng
summary: The repository XML is a local copy of University of Michigan CME item CME00023 and encodes John Hardyng's unfinished Yorkist, or second, version of his verse Chronicle from Henry Ellis's 1812 edition. The XML retains the 1812 title leaf and Richard Grafton's 1543 title, dedication, and preface, then encodes Hardyng's proem and Chronicle through printed page 429; it does not encode Ellis's biographical preface, Grafton's prose continuation on pages 431–607, or the index advertised by the enclosing title. Ellis preferred the fuller of Grafton's two January 1543 prints, now STC 12766.7, noted variants from STC 12767, collated chiefly against British Library Harley MS 661, and incorporated collations from Bodleian MS Arch. Selden. B. 10. A complete 713-page scan of the 1812 volume is cached. Harley 661's cataloged digitized images are currently unavailable; Arch. Selden. B. 10 has only thirteen selected slide images online, all cached as explicitly partial bundles with separate image-license claims.
review_notes: The cataloged intellectual unit is Hardyng's second version, not the complete 1812 printed volume and not Grafton's prose continuation.
The fuller Grafton print is an edition-level base for Ellis; Harley 661 and Arch. Selden. B. 10 are collated witnesses, not interchangeable base manuscripts.
Selected Bodleian slide images and the complete 1812 book scan are reproduction layers with different coverage and rights from the manuscripts and historical texts.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00023
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00023.
The root text declares LANG="enm lat" and the file contains 8 DIV1, 247 DIV2, 13,206 L, 1,931 LG, 4,870 NOTE, 439 PB, and 238 MILESTONE elements.
Excluding L elements nested in editorial NOTE, the work body contains 12,535 encoded verse lines: 287 in the proem and 12,248 in the poem division.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00023
entities.description: A public structured digital text whose bibliographic record names Ellis's 1812 volume but whose encoded matter selects the 1812 and 1543 title leaves, Grafton's dedication and preface, Hardyng's proem, and Hardyng's second-version Chronicle through printed page 429.
notes: The U-M item page applies No Copyright–United States to the digital materials.
The exact original keying date and workflow were not found; the repository XML records a 2016 structural and proofing revision.

entities.label: Ellis, The Chronicle of Iohn Hardyng, 1812
entities.description: Henry Ellis's reset and collated edition of Hardyng's second version together with Richard Grafton's prose continuation, an Ellis biographical and literary preface, and an index.

date_statements.label: title-page publication
date_statements.notes: Ellis dates his closing preface note 3 January 1813, but the edition's title page is 1812.

bibliographic.citation: John Hardyng, The Chronicle of Iohn Hardyng, containing an account of public transactions from the earliest period of English history to the beginning of the reign of King Edward the Fourth, together with the continuation by Richard Grafton, ed. Henry Ellis (London: printed for F. C. and J. Rivington et al., 1812).
bibliographic.title: The Chronicle of Iohn Hardyng
bibliographic.publisher: F. C. and J. Rivington; T. Payne; Wilkie and Robinson; Longman, Hurst, Rees, Orme, and Brown; Cadell and Davies; J. Mawman; R. H. Evans

notes: Ellis identifies Harley MS 661 as the principal manuscript source of his collation and thanks Philip Bliss for principal collations from Arch. Selden. B. 10.
The edition prefers the more copious of Grafton's two January 1543 prints and records the other print's variants in the margin.
Unreferenced square brackets identify words or sentences absent from Harley 661; brackets tied to marginal references display manuscript readings.

entities.label: Grafton, The Chronicle of Ihon Hardyng, 1543 (STC 12766.7)
entities.description: The fuller of Richard Grafton's two January 1543 printings of Hardyng's second version, followed by a lengthy prose continuation through Henry VIII; Ellis chose this print's text for his 1812 edition.

bibliographic.citation: John Hardyng, The chronicle of Ihon Hardyng in metre (London: Richard Grafton, January 1543), STC 12766.7, ESTC S103772.
bibliographic.title: The chronicle of Ihon Hardyng in metre

notes: The Folger vendor record notes a cancel bifolium variant and identifies its EEBO reproduction as Early English Books, 1475–1640, reel 44:1.
Peverley's survey identifies this as the first of Grafton's two prints and the one with the longer continuation.

entities.label: Grafton, The Chronicle of Ihon Hardyng, 1543 (STC 12767)
entities.description: The shorter, revised January 1543 printing, whose continuation omits most of Henry VIII; Ellis treated it as Grafton's duplicate edition and recorded its variants.

bibliographic.citation: John Hardyng, The chronicle of Ihon Hardyng (London: Richard Grafton, January 1543), STC 12767, ESTC S103774.
bibliographic.title: The chronicle of Ihon Hardyng

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 661
entities.description: A richly decorated parchment witness of Hardyng's second version, including genealogical trees, a three-page map of Scotland, appended verses, and the maker's excusation.

physical_description.extent: 1* + 190 folios, with six unfoliated paper flyleaves at the beginning and five at the end
notes: Hardyng's Chronicle occupies fols. 1r–186r; f. 186v is blank.
The map occupies fols. 187r–188v; appended title-to-Scotland verses occupy fols. 188v–189r; the excusation occupies fols. 189r–190r.
Post-1600 binding; the catalog does not enumerate boards, spine, pastedowns, or every flyleaf image in the currently unavailable digital surrogate.
Ellis describes this as the source of his principal manuscript collations.
The current British Library record says digitized content exists but that the images are unavailable.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Arch. Selden. B. 10
entities.description: A parchment composite codex whose first part contains Hardyng's second version and whose later part contains extracts from Lydgate's Fall of Princes.

physical_description.extent: Composite codex: Hardyng part fols. 1–199; later Lydgate part fols. 200–212
notes: The catalog records fine historiated diagrams, borders, initials, and maps in the Hardyng part.
A former silken or velvet cover with brass bosses and gilt leaves has disappeared.
Acquired from John Selden's collection in 1659.
Ellis's preface says many Selden readings agree with Grafton's print and thanks Philip Bliss for the principal collations from this manuscript.
Only thirteen selected slide images were verified online on 2026-07-12; no public whole-codex digital surrogate was found.

entities.label: Harvard–Google–Internet Archive scan of Ellis's 1812 edition
entities.description: A complete 713-page PDF scan of the bound 1812 volume, retaining preliminary leaves, Ellis's preface, Grafton's title and prefatory matter, Hardyng's verse text, Grafton's continuation, index, blanks, and final exterior view.
entities.reproduction_format: Google grayscale page images delivered by Internet Archive as a complete PDF, OCR text derivative, and scan pagination metadata
notes: Representative PDF pages 6, 27–30, 44–47, 54–56, 460–462, 639–640, and 711 were visually inspected.
The scan is monochrome except for the final exterior view; legibility is adequate for the title, preface, apparatus, text boundaries, and index.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected-slide images of Arch. Selden. B. 10
entities.description: Thirteen selected color slide images exposed through four IIIF manifests: ten images in one mixed-part manifest and single-image manifests for Hardyng fols. 184r, 184v, and 185r.
entities.reproduction_format: Color IIIF images digitized from 35mm slides
notes: The ten-image manifest includes Hardyng fols. 5r, 9r, and 15r, two images labeled fol. 198v, and later composite matter at fols. 200r, 201r, 202v, 205r, and 209v.
These are selected slides, not a complete physical-object or complete-leaf facsimile; no covers, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, or continuous folio run is exposed.

entities.label: Sarah Peverley's John Hardyng scholarly survey

bibliographic.citation: Sarah L. Peverley, John Hardyng (University of Liverpool, deposited 2021).
bibliographic.title: John Hardyng

notes: Modeled as a contextual descriptive source so its cached complete PDF, access, and rights remain separate from the historical source chain.

entities.label: Simpson and Peverley, John Hardyng's Chronicle, volume I

bibliographic.citation: John Hardyng, Chronicle, edited from British Library MS Lansdowne 204, ed. James Simpson and Sarah Peverley, vol. 1, Middle English Texts Series (Kalamazoo: Medieval Institute Publications, 2015).
bibliographic.title: John Hardyng's Chronicle, Edited from British Library MS Lansdowne 204, Volume I

notes: This is an edition of Hardyng's first version; it is used only as current scholarship distinguishing the first and second versions, not as an upstream text for CME00023.
The cached publisher PDF is a 37-page preview rather than the complete volume; the complete edited text and introduction are readable through the METS website.

subject.description: Hardyng's second-version verse Chronicle in Ellis's edition.

object.description: The corresponding text in Grafton's shorter revised printing.

scope.notes: Ellis describes this as Grafton's duplicate edition and gives its variations in the margin.

scope.notes: Ellis calls Harley 661 the source of his principal manuscript collations; the edition's brackets encode omissions and manuscript readings according to his stated rules.

subject.description: Hardyng's second-version verse text and Ellis's textual apparatus.

scope.notes: Ellis thanks Philip Bliss for the principal collations from this witness; the later Lydgate part is outside the relation.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two January 1543 printings with differing Hardyng readings and continuations.
editorial_practices.representation: The fuller print supplies Ellis's preferred text; variants from the shorter print appear in the margin.
editorial_practices.description: Ellis states that he preferred the more copious of Grafton's two prints and recorded the other print's variations in the margin.
editorial_practices.scope: Hardyng's second-version Chronicle and the inherited Grafton matter in the 1812 edition.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Omissions and variant readings in Harley 661 and other collated manuscripts.
editorial_practices.representation: Unreferenced brackets mark wording absent from Harley 661; brackets attached to marginal manuscript references present manuscript readings.
editorial_practices.description: Ellis distinguishes words supplied against Harley 661 from positively cited manuscript variants through two uses of square brackets and marginal sigla.
editorial_practices.scope: Hardyng's verse text and apparatus in the 1812 edition.
notes: Ellis identifies Harley 661 as the principal manuscript collation and credits Philip Bliss with the principal Arch. Selden. B. 10 collations.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A composite 1812 volume containing Ellis's modern preface, Grafton prefatory matter, Hardyng's verse Chronicle, Grafton's prose continuation, and an index.
editorial_practices.representation: The encoding retains the two title layers, Grafton's dedication and preface, Hardyng's proem, and Hardyng's Chronicle through printed page 429.
editorial_practices.description: CME00023 selects Hardyng's second-version verse Chronicle and its inherited prefatory matter rather than transcribing the whole 1812 book.
notes: Ellis's xxi-page preface, Grafton's continuation on printed pages 431–607, and the index are outside the encoding.

rights.component: University of Michigan CME digital text and associated public digital materials
rights.basis: The U-M item page applies the standardized No Copyright–United States statement to the digital materials.
notes: This provider statement is recorded separately from the legal status of the fifteenth-century work and the 1812 source edition.

rights.component: Repository XML copy of the U-M CME digital text
rights.basis: The artifact is a pinned local copy of the U-M item whose public record asserts No Copyright–United States; no distinct repository-level license was found in the XML.

rights.component: 1812 printed edition, including Ellis's editorial text and apparatus
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1812, long before the current U.S. public-domain threshold for published works stated in Copyright Office Circular 15A.
The print was published in 1543, long before the current U.S. public-domain threshold for published works.
notes: This does not waive EEBO subscription or provider terms governing access to modern page images.

rights.basis: The print was published in 1543, long before the current U.S. public-domain threshold for published works.
notes: This does not waive EEBO subscription or provider terms governing access to modern page images.

rights.component: Fifteenth-century manuscript text and decoration as historical content
rights.basis: The manuscript was produced c. 1475; the historical content predates copyright, independently of rights or service terms in any modern photography.

rights.component: Any newly supplied British Library digital images
rights.basis: The British Library imaging terms distinguish image supply from permission to reproduce and reserve service conditions for delivered files.
notes: No such images were obtained or cached during this review.

rights.component: Fifteenth- and early-sixteenth-century manuscript text and decoration as historical content
rights.basis: The codex parts date to c. 1470–1480 and c. 1520; their historical content predates copyright, independently of rights in modern slide photography.
The IIIF manifest attributes the slide images to Bodleian Libraries and links a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International license.
Each single-image IIIF manifest attributes the slide to Bodleian Libraries and links a Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 3.0 Unported license.

rights.component: Any newly ordered Bodleian digital images
rights.basis: Bodleian Imaging Services supplies new files for a fee, and reuse remains governed by item-specific rights and Digital Bodleian terms.

rights.component: Google-derived scan, OCR, and pagination files
rights.basis: The underlying 1812 edition is public domain in the United States, but no item-specific open license for the modern Google scan derivatives was found in the Internet Archive metadata.
The institutional repository provides public access, but no item-specific open reuse license was verified in the downloaded PDF.

rights.component: 2015 scholarly edition, METS web text, and publisher preview
rights.basis: The modern edition and publisher preview remain copyrighted; public readability does not itself establish an open reuse license.
The circular is an official work of the United States government.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00023.xml, git commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: Document identifiers, TEI-like header, revision description, and complete XML content
evidence.summary: The repository source identifies CME00023, names Hardyng and Ellis, cites the 1812 edition, declares Middle English and Latin, and records a 2016 structural and proofing revision.

evidence.citation: Structural inventory of Kyvero-Press/CME CME00023.xml at pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: TEXT/FRONT and TEXT/BODY; DIV1 and DIV2 hierarchy; L, LG, NOTE, PB, and MILESTONE elements
evidence.summary: Machine-assisted inspection found 8 DIV1, 247 DIV2, 13,206 L, 1,931 LG, 4,870 NOTE, 439 PB, and 238 MILESTONE elements. Excluding 300 lines nested in notes, the intrinsic work body contains 12,535 lines in 1,807 verse groups: proem 287 lines in 41 groups and poem 12,248 lines in 1,766 groups. The poem contains 246 numbered DIV2 containers and one final unnumbered Scotland itinerary; inherited and inconsistent chapter labels preclude treating this as a canonical count of 247 chapters.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, The Chronicle of Iohn Hardyng, item CME00023.
evidence.locator: Item header, publication statement, stable identifier, and Rights/Permissions section
evidence.summary: The public U-M item names Hardyng's Chronicle and Ellis's 1812 edition, exposes the encoded text under stable identifier CME00023, and applies No Copyright–United States to the digital materials.

evidence.citation: John Hardyng, The Chronicle of Iohn Hardyng, ed. Henry Ellis (London, 1812), complete Harvard–Google scan supplied by Internet Archive.
evidence.locator: 1812 title page; Ellis preface pp. xiv–xxi; Hardyng text through p. 429; Grafton continuation pp. 431–607
evidence.summary: The edition itself establishes its title, publication, contents, chosen fuller Grafton print, duplicate-print marginal variants, principal Harley 661 collation, Philip Bliss's Arch. Selden. B. 10 collations, and the meaning of Ellis's square brackets.

evidence.citation: Open Library, The chronicle of Iohn Hardyng, edition record OL176220M.
evidence.locator: Edition title, publication statement, and pagination
evidence.summary: The bibliographic record supplies the 1812 London imprint and extent of two preliminary leaves, xxi, 607, and 71 unnumbered pages.

evidence.citation: Sarah L. Peverley, John Hardyng (University of Liverpool, deposited 2021), 24 pp.
evidence.locator: Discussion of the two versions, second-version manuscripts, 1543 prints, Ellis 1812, and modern editions
evidence.summary: Peverley distinguishes Hardyng's two versions, dates the first presentation to 1457 and the second version's Yorkist and Edwardian revision, describes the unfinished second version's circulation, identifies its manuscript and print survival, maps STC 12766.7 and 12767 to Grafton's fuller and shorter prints, and cautions that Ellis's 1812 edition is complete but based on the prints and selected manuscript variants.

evidence.citation: James Simpson and Sarah Peverley, eds., John Hardyng's Chronicle, volume I, Middle English Texts Series, online introduction.
evidence.locator: Introduction sections distinguishing first and second versions and describing the second version's state and transmission
evidence.summary: The current scholarly introduction describes the second version as unfinished and over 12,400 lines, notes that its last datable entry cannot predate September 1464, and reports twelve manuscripts plus fragments and two 1543 prints; the volume itself edits the separate first version from Lansdowne 204.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library catalog record 399199, The chronicle of Ihon Hardyng in metre, STC 12766.7, ESTC S103772.
evidence.locator: Title, imprint, extent, identifiers, variant note, and EEBO reproduction note
evidence.summary: The public record identifies the fuller 1543 Grafton print, its STC and ESTC numbers, extent, a cancel-bifolium variant, and its EEBO reel reproduction.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library catalog record 160095, The chronicle of Ihon Hardyng, STC 12767, ESTC S103774.
evidence.locator: Title, imprint, extent, identifiers, and copy description
evidence.summary: The public record identifies the shorter 1543 Grafton print and provides its STC and ESTC numbers, imprint, extent, and Folger copy information.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 661, record 040-002046490.
evidence.locator: Summary, contents, material, dimensions, date, foliation, decoration, provenance, and digital-content notice
evidence.summary: The official record dates the parchment manuscript c. 1475, gives its dimensions and foliation, locates Hardyng's Chronicle and appended matter through fol. 190r, describes its decoration and map, and currently states that its cataloged digital images are unavailable.

evidence.citation: Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Arch. Selden. B. 10, Summary Catalogue 3356.
evidence.locator: Manuscript summary, composite contents, dates, decoration, former binding, provenance, and slide links
evidence.summary: The official Bodleian record describes a composite parchment codex: Hardyng's second version at fols. 1–199, c. 1470–1480, and Lydgate excerpts at fols. 200–212, c. 1520; it also records decoration, the vanished former cover, Selden provenance, and selected slide-image links.
notes: The locator intentionally concerns only MS. Arch. Selden. B. 10; no Royal-manuscript identification is asserted.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for chronicleiohnha00grafgoog, Harvard–Google scan of Ellis's 1812 Chronicle.
evidence.locator: Item metadata and file inventory
evidence.summary: The metadata identifies the Harvard source copy, Google digitization, Internet Archive item, bibliographic source, and downloadable scan derivatives.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of the complete cached Internet Archive PDF for Ellis's 1812 Chronicle.
evidence.summary: Inspection mapped the encoded matter to PDF p. 6, p. 30, pp. 32–44, pp. 46–54, and pp. 56–460; verified the excluded Ellis preface at pp. 7–28, the excluded continuation beginning at p. 462, the index beginning at p. 640, and a final exterior view at p. 711.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries IIIF Presentation manifest for ten selected 35mm slides of MS. Arch. Selden. B. 10.
evidence.locator: Manifest metadata, ten canvases, image services, attribution, and license
evidence.summary: The manifest exposes ten selected slide images rather than a complete codex, labels fols. 5r, 9r, 15r, 198v, 200r, 201r, 202v, 205r, and 209v, and states CC BY-NC 4.0.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries single-image IIIF Presentation manifests for MS. Arch. Selden. B. 10 fols. 184r, 184v, and 185r.
evidence.locator: Manifest metadata, one canvas each, attribution, and license
evidence.summary: The three manifests expose one selected slide each for fols. 184r, 184v, and 185r and state CC BY-NC-SA 3.0.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of cached Digital Bodleian slide bundles for MS. Arch. Selden. B. 10.
evidence.locator: Cached images for fols. 5r, 9r, 15r, 184r, 184v, and 185r
evidence.summary: The sampled images are legible color photographs of decorated parchment leaves and confirm that the IIIF material is selected-leaf photography, not a complete-object sequence.

evidence.citation: Publisher preview PDF for Simpson and Peverley, John Hardyng's Chronicle, volume I (2015).
evidence.locator: 37-page preview file
evidence.summary: The file is a partial publisher preview of the modern first-version edition, not the complete publication and not a source text for CME00023.

evidence.citation: United States Copyright Office, Duration of Copyright, Circular 15A (2026).
evidence.locator: Current public-domain dates for works published in the United States
evidence.summary: The current circular states the statutory duration framework under which works published before 1 January 1931 are in the United States public domain in 2026.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation services.
evidence.locator: Ordering, eligibility, quotation, and delivery information
evidence.summary: The service page establishes that digitisation of collection items can be requested and quoted, subject to item condition, staff assessment, fees, and service constraints.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services terms and conditions.
evidence.locator: Supply, payment, permitted use, and reproduction provisions
evidence.summary: The terms govern ordered image files and distinguish supply of images from permission for further reproduction or publication.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Digital Bodleian terms of use.
evidence.locator: Image reuse, item-specific rights, and noncommercial terms
evidence.summary: The terms explain that item-specific rights statements control; many Digital Bodleian images are offered for noncommercial use, but users must check each item's displayed license.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Order images from Imaging Services.
evidence.locator: Ordering workflow, current minimum charge, and per-image pricing
evidence.summary: The service page provides a request route for new collection photography, states that fees apply, and lists current minimum and per-image charges; complete-volume feasibility remains subject to quotation and collection assessment.

open_questions.question: Which exact exemplars, manuscript sigla, and reading-level dependencies underlie every part of Ellis's 1812 Hardyng text?
next_steps: Transcribe Ellis's complete apparatus and resolve every siglum against current shelfmarks and print-copy records.
Consult A. Hanham's textual studies and Peverley's dissertation or later second-version editorial work for a modern assessment of Ellis's source use.
notes: The manifest records Ellis's explicitly named preferred print, duplicate print, Harley 661, and Arch. Selden. B. 10 without claiming that these exhaust all consulted witnesses.

open_questions.question: When and by what exact keying, proofreading, and transformation workflow was the original U-M CME digital text produced?
next_steps: Ask the University of Michigan Library for legacy project documentation or file provenance for CME00023.
Compare the repository artifact with any export obtainable directly from the current U-M platform.

open_questions.question: Can a complete, current image surrogate of Harley MS 661 be obtained, including the binding, spine, pastedowns, all flyleaves and blanks, fols. 1r–190r, and color or scale targets?
next_steps: Recheck the cataloged Universal Viewer after the British Library restores unavailable digitized images.
Submit a digitisation-services request with an explicit whole-object shot list and ask whether existing master images can be supplied.
notes: The public viewer was unavailable on the review date, so no manuscript image file was cached.

open_questions.question: Can a complete physical-object image surrogate of MS. Arch. Selden. B. 10 be obtained beyond the thirteen selected online slides?
next_steps: Ask Bodleian Imaging Services whether an existing complete master sequence is available.
If new imaging is needed, request both boards, spine, pastedowns, every flyleaf and blank, fols. 1–212, and views documenting the boundary between composite parts.
notes: The cached IIIF bundles remain explicitly partial and do not stand in for a continuous codex surrogate.

open_questions.question: What is the most defensible narrow chronology and place or places for Hardyng's successive revisions of the unfinished second version?
next_steps: Consult Peverley's detailed stemmatic and biographical studies and newer work on the Yorkist and Edwardian revision strata.
Keep revision stages separate from the later dates of surviving manuscript copies.
notes: Current evidence supports revision after the 1457 first-version presentation and continuation into or after September 1464, but not a single secure production place.

open_questions.question: How should the inherited, repeated, and inconsistent chapter numerals in the 1812 source and CME XML map to a canonical second-version chapter sequence?
next_steps: Collate all XML DIV2/@N values and printed headings against the 1543 prints and principal manuscripts.
Avoid publishing a canonical chapter count from the 247 XML containers until that collation is complete.
notes: One final Scotland itinerary is unnumbered, and the inherited headings include repetitions and a container whose XML N does not match its printed roman numeral.
